data.narrative:
QuiX Quantum is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised €15M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across quantum computing: three public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private firms including Quantinuum, IonQ (private rounds), D-Wave Quantum (private rounds), Rigetti Computing (private rounds), and Infleqtion.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The quantum computing sector contains only five same-sector comparables, but zero meet the filtering criteria of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier placement simultaneously. A minimum of three filtered datapoints is required to support peer-median triangulation; relying on all-sector medians without stage and tier alignment would introduce material distortion given the wide dispersion in quantum computing valuations across development phases and business models.

Without a sufficiently deep comparable set meeting stage and scale criteria, traditional triangulation methods cannot be reliably applied. The analysis is therefore constrained: a defensible valuation range cannot be established from the available data. Key uncertainties include the lack of granular financial performance metrics from peers, stage-specific valuation precedents in an emerging sector, and the absence of comparable fundraising multiples at Series A in quantum computing. Further data on recent private fundraising rounds at analogous stages, or expanded peer financial transparency, would be required to support a quantitative valuation conclusion.
